what_it_does: It is a remote-posture registry that classifies MCP tools/operations into postures (safe, design, consent, hazard, locked) and decides which are exposed on a remote MCP gateway; it is fail-closed/deny-by-default and includes an explicitly-denied list.
observations: Top-level _schema is 'remote-posture-registry-v1' and a note declares fail-closed / deny-by-default.
Five postures are defined with an 'expose' boolean and a semantic note.
The tools map contains 22 entries, each with remote_posture, allowed actions, and scope.
Only 'cc_channel' and 'claude_design' use the 'design' posture and have scope 'write'.
Most tools are 'safe' and read-only, with 'allowed' either null or a specific action list.
The 'consent', 'hazard', and 'locked' postures are defined but not assigned to any tool in the tools map.
explicitly_denied lists 15 tools/operations with reasons such as graph mutation, compute invocation, spawning copies, operator-only governance, self-modification, and deferred writes.
The filename carries a '.deleted-20260622' suffix while the content is a complete policy document.
standouts: Only two remote-write paths are exposed: cc_channel.send and claude_design ingest_bundle/submit_seed/walk_decision.
Fail-closed default means any tool not listed as 'safe' or 'design' is not remotely exposed.
explicitly_denied enumerates high-risk capabilities including graph mutation, session spawning, self-modification, and operator governance.
conventions: Per-tool allowed-action whitelist (null when no sub-action filter is specified).
Filename '.deleted-20260622' suggests an archival or deletion naming convention.
concerns: The file is marked deleted but still contains an intact, policy-like registry.
'allowed': null versus an array may be interpreted inconsistently by consumers.
The 'consent' posture is defined but unused in the tools map.
No enforcement mechanism or consumer is referenced inside the file.
Rationale for assigning each tool to a posture is not documented.
notes: This is a strict, fail-closed remote MCP exposure whitelist with only two design-time write paths and a long explicit-deny list.
questions: Which component reads this registry and enforces the remote gateway decisions?
Does the '.deleted-20260622' suffix mean this policy was superseded or removed?
How is 'allowed': null interpreted compared to an empty or full action list?
What process enables a tool classified as 'consent'?
What is the 'Face Pipeline / Claude Design workflow' in concrete terms?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file states it is a fail-closed remote posture registry and it indeed maps tools to exposure postures plus an explicit deny list.
apparent_themes: Separation of safe reads from design writes
intention_signals: To tightly control what an external MCP client can invoke remotely, permitting only safe reads and specific reversible design workflow writes while keeping destructive, operator-only, and self-modifying operations off the remote gateway.
novelty: The 'design' posture for reversible creative workflow writes and the explicit 'deleted-20260622' filename on an otherwise complete policy document.
contribution: It contributes a concrete, enumerated remote exposure policy with a posture taxonomy and deny-by-default guardrails that can inform access-control decisions in a fused system.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON remote-posture registry that whitelists MCP tools for remote exposure under fail-closed rules, allowing mostly safe reads plus two design workflow write paths and explicitly denying destructive, operator-only, and self-modifying operations.
intention_for_embedding: To define which MCP tools and operations may be exposed on a remote gateway, restricting remote callers to safe reads and designated reversible design actions.
